title: Falcaria longiloba
intro: Falcaria longiloba, also known as the long-stalked carrot fern or dragon's tongue, is a plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, making it a popular subject for research and herbal medicine. The bioactive constituents of Falcaria longiloba include falcarinol, a sesquiterpenoid compound with demonstrated anti-inflammatory activity, as well as other alkaloids and saponins. Herbal preparations of Falcaria longiloba are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are traditionally used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including fever and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Falcaria longiloba is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Falcaria, which comprises a small group of plants. Falcaria longiloba is native to Europe and Western Asia, and its classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae. The plant's classification is a reflection of its evolutionary relationships with other species within the family Apiaceae. This classification helps scientists understand the plant's characteristics and behaviors.
